dossier.overview:
**Overworld Pokémon spawn** answers only: which Pokémon appears, where, when, and how consistently.

**Weighted natural spawning:** maps define tags, capacity, type affinities, and no-spawn zones; the resolver picks candidates and tiles. No fixed "Bulbasaur at tile 12,8" for normal guests.

**Out of scope:** roaming behavior, smart objects, activities, dialogue, partner follow, staff job loops. Those systems consume spawned actors later. See **Overworld NPC AI** for decision architecture after spawn.

Inputs are game-native only: typing, Nature, Characteristic, size/weight class, availability, player placement assignment. Do not hand-author per-species favorite places.

sections.title: Purpose and scope
sections.summary: Spawn owns population and placement. Not behavior.

blocks.title: Spawn pipeline
blocks.caption: Designers author constraints; the system picks Pokémon and tiles
blocks.source:
flowchart TD
    AVAIL["Available Resort Pokemon"] --> PROFILE["Map spawn profile"]
    PROFILE --> SNAP{"Warm snapshot?"}
    SNAP -->|yes| RESTORE["Restore continuity"]
    SNAP -->|no| SCORE["Score candidates"]
    RESTORE --> SCORE
    SCORE --> FILL["Fill capacity priority order"]
    FILL --> TILES["Score valid tiles"]
    TILES --> PICK["Weighted random tile"]
    PICK --> SAVE["Presence and spawn snapshot"]

blocks.html:
This system ownsNot owned here
Map population selection, eligibility, timing, scoringRoaming, smart objects, minigames
Tile placement, no-spawn zones, capacityFriendship activities, dialogue
Player map assignment, offstage visibilityPartner follow, staff job behavior
Warm/cold reload continuity, spawn snapshotsMulti-Pokemon interaction logic

sections.title: Natures
sections.summary: 25 game Natures in six spawn groups. Missing Nature = no modifier.

blocks.html:
Most Natures raise one stat and lower another; five are neutral. For spawn, map tags apply group modifiers (not per-Nature tiles).

Nature groupNaturesSpawn tendency
assertiveLonely, Brave, Adamant, NaughtyOpen areas, training-like areas, visible edges, less passive areas
steadyBold, Relaxed, Impish, LaxRest areas, quiet areas, shade, familiar zones
curiousModest, Mild, Quiet, RashPOI-dense maps, gardens, shops, unusual areas
composedCalm, Gentle, Sassy, CarefulScenic areas, calm public spaces, organized maps
energeticTimid, Hasty, Jolly, NaivePaths, beaches, plazas, play-friendly maps, active spaces
neutralHardy, Docile, Serious, Bashful, QuirkyBalanced / default

blocks.caption: Map tag to Nature group modifiers (clamp -25 to +25)

blocks.body: Valid Nature strings for spawn parsing. Invalid or missing Nature: Pokémon may still spawn with zero Nature affinity.

blocks.caption: All 25 Natures and their spawn group

sections.title: Characteristics
sections.summary: 30 fixed in-game phrases (IV-derived). Flavor bias, weaker than typing.

blocks.body: Characteristic is the summary phrase ("Likes to run", "Highly curious"). Parser should accept known aliases from older games. Contribution tiers: strong +15 to +30, medium +5 to +15, minor -5 to +8.

blocks.html:

Time blockHours
dawn05:00 to 07:00

Late night means more offstage/rest, not an empty resort.

blocks.caption: Characteristic spawn modifiers by time block

sections.title: Map authoring and placement
sections.summary: Constraints and zones, not fixed guest pins.

blocks.caption: Map spawn profile (tags, capacity, type affinity)

blocks.html:
Exact spawn policy

  • Normal guests: weighted natural placement only.
  • Player residents: map/section anchor; tile still varies.
  • Staff/story: may use exact authored anchors (exceptions).

Dual-type affinity: primary + secondary * 0.75. Water typing raises beach/pool map score; placement stays on valid land/edge tiles (never spawn in water by default).

Hard no-spawn (never relax): collision, structures, walls, warps, doors, player spawn, cutscene/battle triggers, smart-object entrances and role points, one-tile chokes, occupied tiles, too close to player on load.

RuleDefault tiles
Min distance from player on load8
Min distance from map entry5
Min distance from building door4
Min distance from warp4
Min distance from smart-object entrance3
Min distance between Pokémon3

If the map is small, relax Pokémon spacing first. Never relax collision, warp, structure, or player tile.

Soft-avoid (score penalty only): main paths, near-door buffers, connectors, camera-critical tiles, crowding. Pokémon can still appear on paths sometimes.

blocks.caption: Tile score formula (after Pokémon selected for map)
blocks.body:
tileScore =
  baseTileScore
  + sectionAffinity
  + mapTagAffinity
  + typeTileAffinity
  + natureTileAffinity
  + characteristicTileAffinity
  + playerAssignmentSectionBonus
  + spacingBonus
  - softAvoidPenalty
  - crowdingPenalty
  - playerProximityPenalty

clamp 0..100, then weighted-random pick tile

**Placement fallback order:** relax distance from other Pokémon, then soft-avoid, then player distance (never below 3 tiles), try another section, skip Pokémon if impossible, try another candidate if below minimum population. Never relax collision, warp, door, structure, occupied tile, player tile, or hard no-spawn zones.

sections.title: Spawn scoring and fill order
sections.summary: Who appears on the map, in what priority.

blocks.caption: Pokémon spawn score formula
blocks.body:
spawnScore =
  baseAvailability
  + typeAffinity
  + natureGroupAffinity
  + characteristicAffinity
  + characteristicTimeAffinity
  + playerAssignmentBonus
  + friendAlreadySelectedBonus
  + spawnDebtBonus
  - recentSpawnPenalty
  - offstageRestPenalty
  - overcrowdingPenalty

clamp 0..100, weighted random (not always max)

blocks.html:
Population fill order

  1. Restore warm-map Pokémon if continuity requires
  2. Required exact-anchor Pokémon (special cases)
  3. Player always_present assignments
  4. Player resident assignments (reserve slots)
  5. Player soft assignments if they score well
  6. Fill remaining capacity with natural weighted candidates
  7. Minimum population fallback
  8. Save presence records
Placement typeMeaningSpawn bonus
softPlayer suggested this area+30
residentUsually on this map/section+60
always_presentFind here whenever available+100

blocks.caption: Player placement assignment (not a favorite place)

blocks.html:
Spawn timing: pass on map load, cold reload, time block change (if map not hot), population refresh, assignment change, return from away. Not every frame, not in front of the player on hot visible maps (stage offscreen/edge entry).

Away timeSpawn result
0 to 20 sPreserve exact population and positions
20 to 90 sSame Pokémon, small placement correction
90 s to 5 minSame population likely, positions refreshed nearby
5 to 15 minPartial preserve, some reroll
15 min or moreFull population refresh allowed

Offstage minimum durations (reduces spawn score until elapsed): resting 10 min, in_room 15 min, private_wander 8 min, late_night_rest until next time block, population_refresh 15 min, recently_visible_cooldown 5 min.

Spawn debt: +5 per missed eligible refresh, max +25. Recent spawn penalty: -20 within 900 s (reduced for assigned residents).

blocks.caption: Recommended overworldPokemonSpawning config shape
Spawn snapshot on map unload (not behavior state)

blocks.html:
Acceptance criteria

  • No spawn on invalid tiles, doors, warps, or player spawn
  • Typing strongly influences map fit; Nature moderate; Characteristic light to moderate plus time
  • Resident assignment and reserve slots protect player choices
  • Sleepy Pokémon deprioritized in busy morning when alternatives exist; still spawn if map would be empty
  • Brief leave preserves population; long absence allows refresh
  • Normal guests use natural tiles, not designer pins
  • Seeded randomness: mapId, timeBlock, resortDay, pkrid, spawnRefreshIndex
